done.journal-03: 2026-07-22: Built + validated the journal-analyze n8n workflow (n8n/journal-analyze.workflow.json), superseding the journal-webhook-probe as owner of POST /webhook/journal. Pipeline: webhook -> loop-safe Guard (Code) proceeds only on memos.memo.created carrying #journal, drops memos.memo.comment.created and skips its own reflection (sentinel), so writing a comment (which fires TWO events, both carrying the comment memo) never re-triggers -> HTTP to rig llama-swap :9292 (dolphin-venice-24b, temp 0.35, 120s timeout, 3 retries, continue-on-error) -> tolerant JSON parse/repair (strips fences, first-brace, closes truncated objects) -> write-back as a Memos COMMENT (never a body edit). Config via the n8n container env (MEMOS_API_TOKEN/LLM_MODEL/LLM_BASE_URL/LLM_TEMP; N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE=false added to compose) so the coach model is a one-line .env swap. Ships templates/{daily,gamelog}.md (both #journal). 15/15 deterministic unit tests on the Guard + JSON-repair. E2E validated: a #journal and a #gamelog memo each yielded EXACTLY ONE reflection comment (mood 4/10 neg; mood 8/10 pos) and the comment events were skipped (n8n exec trace: the memo.created posted; the comment.created + comment-memo.created ran Guard-only, no post). Validation LLM run used fast-3b as a VRAM-fitting stand-in (Immich ML held the GPU; per operator, do not evict Immich) so dolphin-venice-24b reflection QUALITY is the only bit deferred to when the rig GPU frees; the mechanism/loop-guard/parse/write-back are proven. n8n 2.x gotcha: needs publish:workflow + restart (not just active=1); CLI unpublish leaves a stale webhook_entity row (cleared with n8n stopped). New crit check journaling-analyze-armed (GET /webhook/journal must say not-registered-for-GET) -> 6/6 journaling checks green. Coverage unchanged (no new container).
done.journal-04: 2026-07-23: Wired the optional server-side faster-whisper transcription branch into journal-analyze (n8n/journal-analyze.workflow.json). On memos.memo.created the Guard reads memo.attachments[] (carried INLINE in the webhook payload, snake_case), and an IF node routes the first audio/* attachment through Fetch (Memos /file/{attachment}/{filename}, Bearer PAT) -> Transcribe (faster-whisper container-port :8000 /v1/audio/transcriptions, multipart, model Systran/faster-whisper-small on CPU) -> Merge (appends '[Voice note transcript]' to the entry + rebuilds the LLM messages) BEFORE the coach LLM; a text-only memo skips the whole branch (IF->false, Fetch/Transcribe/Merge never execute). Fail-safe: both HTTP nodes are continue-on-error and Merge falls back to the Guard's text-only messages, so whisper being down never blocks or regresses the primary text path; Guard also relaxes its empty-body drop when audio is present so a voice-only #journal entry is still analyzed. New non-secret swappable env WHISPER_BASE_URL/WHISPER_MODEL added to compose + .env(.example) (node expressions default to them, so unset still works). Memos 0.29 fact: resources are ATTACHMENTS (POST /api/v1/attachments; binary at GET /file/{name}/{filename}); the webhook memo carries attachments[] inline so the text path pays nothing. Deployed import:workflow + publish:workflow + compose recreate; webhook re-armed clean (one webhook_entity row, GET says not-registered-for-GET). 21/21 JS unit tests on Guard+Merge. E2E: a #journal memo with a spoken .wav was transcribed and its reflection referenced content that appears ONLY in the audio (pomegranate/hopeful) with EXACTLY ONE comment (loop guard held on the 2 comment-events); a text-only memo skipped the branch. LLM run used the fast-3b VRAM-fitting stand-in (dolphin-venice-24b OOMs while Immich ML holds the rig GPU, ~12.9GB free) then restored to dolphin-venice-24b as production. New daily consumer check journaling-whisper-transcribes (real transcription of a bundled probe WAV, not just /health; asset in verification/assets/) -> 7/7 journaling checks green. Coverage unchanged (faster-whisper already counted in journal-01; no new container). Wiki enrichment + troubleshoot updated. journal-05/06 remain.
